skill.name: working-with-documents
skill.description: Guides document creation, editing, conversion, and validation for Word, PDF, and PowerPoint files.
skill.summary: Create, inspect, convert, and revise DOCX, PDF, and PPTX documents.
skill.author: Asmayaseen

security_audit.summary: The high-severity static findings are false positives caused by document terms, XML examples, and simple validation text. The skill does contain legitimate shell-command and file-write guidance, so it should publish with a medium-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Local shell command guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends local commands for conversion, package installation, unpacking, packing, and validation. These commands are relevant to document workflows, but they can modify files or install software.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are explicit and numerous. The context is legitimate document processing, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: False positive blocker detections
low_findings.description: The static blocker findings for weak cryptography, command-and-control keywords, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance were not confirmed. No evidence found of cryptography, C2 behavior, host profiling, or network scanning.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows ordinary document descriptions, PDF merge examples, and validation checks. These lines do not implement malicious or reconnaissance behavior.

low_findings.title: Document file write examples
low_findings.description: The skill includes examples that write DOCX and PDF outputs to local files. This matches the skill purpose, but users should avoid overwriting important files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are direct and visible. They create expected document outputs and do not target sensitive paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package installation command examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The dependency section includes pip, apt-get, and npm install commands. These are not malicious, but package installation should be reviewed before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly present and install third-party tools. The packages align with document processing and do not show hidden behavior.

content.value_statement: Document workflows often require different tools for DOCX, PDF, and PPTX files. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical guidance for creation, conversion, editing, and validation.
actual_capabilities: Read DOCX content with pandoc or python-docx guidance.
Create DOCX files using docx-js patterns.
Extract, merge, split, and create PDF files with Python and command-line tools.
Create and validate PPTX presentations through HTML and OOXML workflows.
Convert between DOCX, PPTX, PDF, Markdown, and image outputs.
Perform OCR guidance for scanned PDF documents.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a bundled document engine.
Some workflows require external tools such as pandoc, qpdf, LibreOffice, or poppler.
OOXML editing requires care because invalid XML can corrupt Office files.
OCR quality depends on scan clarity, language, and installed OCR packages.

use_cases.title: Prepare client-ready reports
use_cases.description: Generate structured DOCX or PDF reports from source text, tables, and review comments.

use_cases.title: Automate document conversion
use_cases.description: Convert files between Office, PDF, Markdown, and image formats during repeatable workflows.

use_cases.title: Revise presentation decks
use_cases.description: Create, inspect, reorder, and validate PowerPoint slides using reproducible commands.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple document
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DOCX outline for this topic. Include headings, short paragraphs, and a clear file creation plan.

prompt_templates.title: Extract PDF content
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the text and tables from this PDF. Summarize the structure and flag pages that need manual review.

prompt_templates.title: Convert and validate files
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a repeatable workflow to convert these DOCX files to PDF and Markdown. Include validation steps and expected outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Edit OOXML safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a safe OOXML edit for this Office file. Identify files to unpack, changes to make, validation checks, and rollback steps.

output_examples.input: Create a three-section project brief as a DOCX file.
output_examples.output: A document plan with title, overview, milestones, risks, and a recommended DOCX generation approach.

output_examples.input: Merge two PDF reports and extract the first five pages.
output_examples.output: A concise workflow describing merge steps, page selection, output names, and validation checks.

output_examples.input: Reorder slides in a presentation and verify the result.
output_examples.output: A slide rearrangement plan with the new order, validation thumbnails, and checks for speaker notes.

best_practices: Work on copies before editing Office XML or splitting PDFs.
Validate generated documents by opening them and checking page or slide counts.
Use explicit input and output filenames to avoid accidental overwrites.
anti_patterns: Do not run package installation commands without reviewing the packages first.
Do not edit packed Office files without unpacking and validating their XML structure.
Do not assume OCR output is accurate without spot-checking difficult pages.

faq.question: Does this skill create documents directly?
faq.answer: It provides workflows and examples for creating documents with supported local tools.

faq.question: Which file formats does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers DOCX, PDF, PPTX, Markdown, images, and scanned PDF text extraction.

faq.question: Can it preserve tracked changes?
faq.answer: It explains pandoc and OOXML patterns for reviewing or representing tracked changes.

faq.question: Does it require external software?
faq.answer: Many workflows need tools such as pandoc, qpdf, LibreOffice, poppler, or Python packages.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive documents?
faq.answer: Use local processing when possible and review commands before running them on confidential files.

faq.question: Can it handle scanned PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes OCR guidance using image conversion and pytesseract.
